mission.title: kubearmor: Index out of bound on containers with short names
mission.description: Index out of bound on containers with short names. Users encounter: "runtime error: slice bounds out of range".

steps.title: Identify kubearmor troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kubearmor deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubearmor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubearmor
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubearmor -n kubearmor --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `runtime error: slice bounds out of range`

steps.title: Review kubearmor configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubearmor configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubearmor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubearmor
kubectl get configmap -n kubearmor -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubearmor
```
## Bug Report

I've spawned kuberarmor on a kubernetes cluster, but noticed one pod constantly dying with the error:
```
panic: runtime error: slice bounds out of range 
```
After some hours of despair while debugging I found out that a container

steps.title: Apply the fix for Index out of bound on containers with short names
steps.description:
Hey , You are correct that the logic for verifying that the container ID is at least 12 characters long is flawed, which is why KubeArmor is not functioning properly. We are working on resolving this issue.
```yaml
panic: runtime error: slice bounds out of range
```

steps.title: Confirm Index out of bound on containers with short names is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubearmor -n kubearmor --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kubearmor --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `runtime error: slice bounds out of range` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary: Hey , You are correct that the logic for verifying that the container ID is at least 12 characters long is flawed, which is why KubeArmor is not functioning properly. We are working on resolving this issue.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubearmor installed or the issue environment reproducible.
